title: Negotiations for the Rivermark Compromise
content:
The Rivermark Compromise (1836) was a diplomatic agreement in which Ulric Jardine, chief negotiator of Confederation of Frosthaven, helped finalize terms at Lunaris.

Negotiations.

Ulric Jardine, serving as chief negotiator, chaired preliminary talks that began in spring 1835. Neutral observers from neighboring cantons attended the sessions.

Background.

By the early 1836s, diplomats from Confederation of Frosthaven sought a settlement after years of border incidents near Lunaris.

Historical assessment.

Modern historians view the Rivermark Compromise as a stabilizing milestone, crediting Ulric Jardine's insistence on verifiable inspection clauses.

Implementation.

Commission reports from 1837 show gradual demobilization and the reopening of overland routes through the Lunaris basin.

Reactions.

Newspapers in Confederation of Frosthaven praised the compromise, while opposition leaders argued that naval access points near Lunaris were not sufficiently protected.
